betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Accelerator Vents: Empty Cooler & Passive Cooling§f

§aThe Empty Cooler is the foundation block for every QMD accelerator cooler!§f
The §bEmpty Cooler§f (`qmd:part:0`, ore dict `empty_cooler`) is an intermediate item that must be combined with various materials to produce all 32 §bAccelerator Cooler§f blocks. Coolers are placed on the surface of LINAC, Synchrotron, Beam Diverter, Beam Splitter, and Decelerator multiblocks to dissipate heat generated by accelerating particles.

§6Empty Cooler Crafting Recipe§f:
Per `QMDCraftingRecipeHandler.java:102`:
- Output: §b8 × Empty Cooler§f
- Pattern (3×3): §bS-T-S / S-H-S / S-T-S§f
  - §bS§f = §bStainless Steel Ingot§f
  - §bT§f = §bTough Alloy Ingot§f
  - §bH§f = §bThermoconducting Alloy Ingot§f

§6Cooler Variants (Tier 1 — `acceleratorCooler1`)§f:
Right-click an Empty Cooler with a §bWater Bucket§f, or surround it with materials in a 3×3 pattern, to obtain:
- §bWater§f, §bIron§f, §bRedstone§f, §bQuartz§f, §bObsidian§f, §bNether Brick§f, §bGlowstone§f, §bLapis§f
- §bGold§f, §bPrismarine§f, §bSlime§f, §bEnd Stone§f, §bPurpur§f, §bDiamond§f, §bEmerald§f, §bCopper§f

§6Cooler Variants (Tier 2 — `acceleratorCooler2`)§f:
Same pattern but with higher-tier materials:
- §bTin§f, §bLead§f, §bBoron§f, §bLithium§f, §bMagnesium§f, §bManganese§f, §bAluminum§f, §bSilver§f
- §bFluorite§f, §bVilliaumite§f, §bCarobbiite§f, §bArsenic§f
- §bLiquid Nitrogen§f, §bLiquid Helium§f, §bCryotheum§f (bucket + cooler shapeless)

§6Cooling Rate§f:
The cooling power of each cooler type is defined per-variant in `QMDInfo.cooler1FixedInfo()` / `cooler2FixedInfo()` using `CoolerType.getHeatRemoved()`. Cryotheum and Liquid Helium are the most powerful passive coolers; Water and Iron are the weakest.

§eTip§f: Each cooler block you place on an accelerator multiblock wall removes heat continuously, so over-cool a small LINAC first to learn the rate progression. A full LINAC may need a mix of Liquid Nitrogen and Cryotheum coolers on its inner walls to keep up with high-energy Boron or Antiproton beams.
